title: pilea tetraphylla var. hypnophila
intro: Pilea tetraphylla var. hypnophila, also known as Nepalese algebra plant or Nepalese ironwort, is a type of flowering plant that belongs to the Urticaceae family. It has been found to possess medicinal properties, primarily due to the presence of iron and other minerals, which have been used in traditional medicine to treat anemia, fatigue, and other iron-deficiency related conditions. The health benefits of this plant include its ability to increase red blood cell count, improve oxygenation of the body, and boost overall energy levels. In traditional herbal preparations, the leaves of Pilea tetraphylla var. hypnophila are often made into a tea or infused in water to extract its medicinal properties, which can also be used as a natural remedy for iron deficiency.
